headlines.title: FISA spying power scheduled to expire as House fails to extend it before leaving town
headlines.description: FISA Section 702, a warrantless surveillance tool, is set to lapse as Congress fights with President Donald Trump about Bill Pulte, the acting Director of National Intelligence.

headlines.title: Federal authorities investigate giant ‘8647’ markings on National Mall
headlines.description: A judge ruled this month that the numbers on their own, which the Trump administration has called a threat against the president, constitute free speech.

headlines.title: Third batch of declassified UFO files reveals sightings around the world were investigated
headlines.description: The Pentagon on Friday released a third batch of vintage classified files related to “flying saucers” and other unidentified anomalous phenomena — better known to most Americans as unidentified flying objects, or UFOs — that the government has been hanging on to for decades

headlines.title: Ohio progressive group searched by FBI accuses Trump’s DOJ of intimidation
headlines.description: FBI special agents searched an office of a progressive organization in Ohio on Thursday as part of a Justice Department investigation into its voter registration efforts, three people familiar with the matter told NBC News

headlines.title: Judge in Tyler Robinson’s case to weigh holding prosecutors in contempt
headlines.description: The man accused of assassinating Charlie Kirk will be back in court on Friday, where a judge is expected to decide whether to hold prosecutors in contempt for comments made about the case on Fox News

headlines.title: Iranian state media says reports of a deal are 'speculative and nothing has been finalized'
headlines.description: After President Trump announced that U.S. strikes on Iran would be canceled due to a deal to end the war being signed in the next few days, Iranian state media responded that the reports of a deal are "speculative and nothing has been finalized." NBC News' Monica Alba, Matt Bradley and Brian Cheung report on the implications politically, abroad and on the stock market.

headlines.title: N.Y. House candidate Micah Lasher reacts to Jack Schlossberg saying he's pushing the 'status quo'
headlines.description: A crowded field of candidates are battling it out to fill retiring Rep. Jerry Nadler’s (D-N.Y.) seat. New York State Assemblyman Micah Lasher (D) joins Meet the Press NOW to discuss his bid for the open House seat.

headlines.title: Netanyahu says Israel 'not a party' to U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding as Trump touts deal
headlines.description: President Trump says he reached a “great settlement” with Iran that is “subject to finalization.” NBC News correspondents Gabe Gutierrez, Richard Engel, Courtney Kube and Brian Cheung join Meet the Press NOW to report on the latest developments.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: Map: How much you pay in federal and state gas taxes
headlines.description: As national gas price averages head toward $5 per gallon, one state has already suspended its gas tax, and now President Donald Trump and his Cabinet are talking about doing the same.

headlines.title: Democratic senators question DOJ decision to drop case against billionaire
headlines.description: The Trump administration said it wanted to drop its criminal fraud charges against Gautam Adani, one of the world’s wealthiest people. A judge hasn’t yet signed off.

headlines.title: U.K. defense secretary resigns, saying the government isn’t willing to spend enough on the military
headlines.description: The resignation dealt another blow to embattled Prime Minister Keir Starmer, who already faces demands from Labour colleagues to step down.
